Ad Text:
About Prospection

Prospection is a healthcare analytics company that partners with life sciences organisations, healthcare providers, and government agencies to deliver actionable insights from large-scale, longitudinal healthcare datasets.

We specialise in real-world data (RWD) working across prescription, claims, and other healthcare datasets to help our clients better understand patient journeys, treatment dynamics, and healthcare outcomes.

Operating across Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and the United States, Prospection combines deep healthcare domain expertise with advanced analytics and technology to solve complex commercial, clinical, and policy challenges. As a scale-up, we value ownership, pragmatism, and impact - building differentiated solutions while continuously evolving how we deliver value to our clients.

The Opportunity

Prospection is seeking a Head of Client Solutions, ANZ to lead client engagement, revenue growth, and solution delivery across Australia and New Zealand.

This is a senior, hybrid role that sits at the intersection of sales, account management, solution design, and delivery execution. The role is intentionally balanced:

~50% Sales/Account Management & Leadership
~50% Solutioning & Delivery


The role will work closely with Prospection’s Centre of Excellence (CoE) in India, translating client needs into clear analytical requirements and ensuring high-quality, scalable delivery.

This position is ideal for someone who is commercially minded, analytically grounded, and comfortable operating in a fast-moving, high-accountability environment.

Responsibilities: 


Sales & Account Management (≈50%)

Own and grow a portfolio of ANZ clients across pharma, biotech, and healthcare organisations
Lead new business development efforts, including opportunity identification, proposal development, and deal closure
Build strong, trusted relationships with senior client stakeholders
Manage expectations, scope, and commercial outcomes across long-term client engagements
Act as the primary point of accountability for client satisfaction and retention

Solutioning & Delivery Leadership (≈50%)

Lead solution design in response to client problems, combining business context, healthcare knowledge, and analytics
Translate client questions into structured analytical briefs for the India CoE
Oversee delivery execution to ensure outputs are timely, accurate, and commercially relevant
Provide strategic guidance and quality assurance on insights and recommendations
Continuously improve delivery processes to enhance scalability, efficiency, and consistency

To be successful in this role you will have:

Experience & Background

Healthcare or pharmaceutical industry experience preferred

3+ years’ experience working with healthcare datasets, such as claims, prescription, hospital, or related real-world data
Proven experience in client-facing roles, including sales, account management, consulting, or solution delivery
Demonstrated experience leading and managing teams, either directly or in a matrix environment

Core Capabilities

Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build credibility at multiple organisational levels
Commercial acumen — comfortable discussing value, pricing, and ROI with clients
Ability to quickly learn business nuances and adapt communication style accordingly
Analytical mindset, with the ability to frame problems clearly even if not hands-on in analysis
Confident working across geographies and cultures, particularly with offshore delivery teams

Personal Attributes

Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shaping solutions rather than following templates
Consultative and persuasive, with the ability to commercialise solutions rather than just execute tasks
Pragmatic, outcomes-focused, and accountable
Collaborative leader who elevates both clients and internal teams

Benefits 

Flexible working arrangements.
Opportunity to shape and grow the ANZ business in a high-impact role.
A runaway to accelerate career growth.
Opportunity to participate in healthcare events across the country.

Prospection Vision & Values

Integrity - Always do the right thing 

Collaboration – Everyone plays, everyone contributes

Customer focus – Get to the heart of the matter, create impact beyond expectations

Innovation – Bring the future into the present

Expert – Be the “go to” person


We respectfully acknowledge the Traditional Owners of the land on which we work and learn, and pay respect to the First Nations Peoples and their elders, past, present and future.

New SAP project



We are seeking an experienced SAP Datasphere Data Modeller / Data Engineer to design, build, and optimise SAP domain data models and data pipelines supporting analytics and reporting use cases. This role will play a critical part in delivering trusted, high-performance consumption-layer models on SAP Datasphere, integrating data from SAP S/4HANA and related SAP systems into a modern analytics architecture, with downstream consumption via enterprise BI platforms such as Tableau Cloud and Power BI.

The ideal candidate will bring strong hands-on experience in SAP Datasphere with deep knowledge of SAP data structures, and a solid background in SAP HANA and/or SAP BW data modelling. Experience across core SAP business domains including Finance, Sales and Distribution, Manufacturing, Logistics, and Procurement is essential.



Responsibilities:


Design and develop analytical and consumption‑layer data models in SAP Datasphere for reporting, dashboards, and advanced analytics, consumed via Tableau Cloud and Power BI.
Apply best‑practice dimensional and semantic modelling to deliver performant, scalable, business‑friendly data structures.
Build reusable, standardised data models and views aligned with enterprise architecture and governance standards.
Collaborate with business SMEs, functional consultants, and analytics teams to translate requirements into robust data models.
Use SQL and SAP Datasphere capabilities to implement transformations, calculations, and business logic.
Optimise query performance and data refresh strategies for analytics workloads.
Ensure data quality, lineage, and traceability across end‑to‑end data pipelines.
Support downstream analytics consumption across platforms including Snowflake, Tableau Cloud, and Power BI.
Contribute to modelling standards, naming conventions, and development guidelines for Datasphere and SAP analytics.
Assist with testing, validation, and deployment of data models across DEV, TEST, and PROD environments.



Essential Skills


Proven hands‑on experience with SAP Datasphere as a data modeller and/or data engineer.
Strong background in SAP HANA modelling (Calculation Views, SQL Script) and/or SAP BW/BW/4HANA.
Deep understanding of SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP data structures, tables, and relationships.
Demonstrated experience across core SAP domains: Finance, Sales & Distribution, Manufacturing/PP, Logistics & Inventory (MM‑IM), and Procurement (MM‑PUR).
Strong SQL skills with experience building complex transformations and analytical logic.
Experience designing star schemas, fact/dimension models, and semantic layers for analytics.
Proven ability to integrate SAP S/4HANA data into analytics platforms via Datasphere.
Experience designing and optimising models for consumption by Tableau Cloud and/or Power BI.
Solid understanding of data warehousing, performance optimisation, and data governance principles.
Desirable exposure to SAP HANA hybrid landscapes, S/4HANA Embedded Analytics/CDS views, data integration tools (DP Agent, Cloud Connector), and large‑scale S/4HANA transformation programs.

Job Title: Project Manager- Australian Research Data Commons Projects
Ad Text:
Partnering with Australian universities, the Australian Research Data Commons (ARDC) is a transformational initiative that is enabling the Australian research community and industry access to nationally significant, leading-edge infrastructure, platforms, skills and collections of high-quality data.

The ARDC has extensive experience in co-designing and delivering national digital research infrastructure programs, and the UNSW based Project Managers are part of the Program Design and Delivery team, supporting the ARDC’s strategic objectives in optimising and expanding the capability of delivering the thematic research data commons across HASS and Indigenous, People, and Planet Research.

Primarily focused on the People Research Data Commons (health and medical research infrastructure), you will apply your expertise to support the delivery of multiple large, national research data projects within ARDC’s Data Commons programs. Working closely with Program Managers, the Project Management office (PMO), and a range of partners, you will be responsible for planning, delivering, and monitoring projects, including scoping, budgeting, progress reporting, and resolving challenges. 

About UNSW:

UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. We are a large organisation with a diverse and highly talented community doing extraordinary work. We are driven to be thoughtful, practical and purposeful in all we do, and this collective approach is what gives our work impact. It is also why UNSW is consistently ranked among the world’s top 20 universities in the QS World University Rankings and is a proud member of Australia’s prestigious Group of Eight. If you’re seeking a career where you can thrive, be challenged and contribute to meaningful work, you’re in the right place. 

Benefits and Culture:

People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Career development opportunities 
17% superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)
Affordable on-campus parking

Who You Are:

You have demonstrated experience in project management, including project scoping and development, monitoring budgets, reporting on project progress and identifing project challenges to identify and apply solutions. The role requires strong understanding of data‑sharing practices that support secondary data use (including FAIR principles) and familiarity of the challenges involved in developing and operating research infrastructure, particularly in relation to health and/or medical research data.

You have a solid understanding of the project lifecycle, complemented by well-developed relationship management, consulting, and communication skills. Your ability to think analytically and strategically enables you to navigate complex, dynamic environments and manage competing priorities effectively. You are experienced in working with distributed teams and collaborators, and can demonstrate your commitment to UNSW’s values, policies, and health and safety responsibilities.

Experience in the medical or health data field is highly desirable. 

How to Apply:

Please submit your CV and a Cover Letter that clearly addresses the required Skills and Experience, using specific examples from your work history before 11.55pm, Sunday 12th April 2026. A copy of the Position Description can be found on JOBS@ UNSW.

Pre-Employment Checks
Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks: - 

National and International Criminal history checks
Entitlement to work and ID checks
Working With Children Checks
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
Verification of relevant qualifications
Verification of relevant professional membership
Employment history and reference checks
Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
Medical Checks and Assessments

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.

Contact:  Shiree Thomas, Talent Acquisition Partner e: shiree.thomas@unsw.edu.au

Please apply through the application portal and not via the contact above.

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions. 

With millions of customer interactions across our ecosystem and a uniquely rich dataset spanning customer behaviour, travel and partner engagement, data plays a central role in how we personalise experiences and make decisions at scale.

About the role:

We’re looking for a Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics Centre of Excellence. In this role, you’ll build, deploy and scale machine learning and experimentation solutions that directly shape loyalty strategy, customer decisioning and personalisation, partner performance, and customer experience across the Velocity ecosystem.

You’ll work in a cross functional environment alongside Insight Analysts, Analytics Engineers and business stakeholders, owning problems end to end from framing and experiment design through to production deployment and measurable business impact, embedding models into production systems that power large scale customer and member decisioning.

You’ll report to the Manager, Personalisation Analytics and Data Science and join a team with established models in production, including recommender systems, propensity and regression models, supported by an evolving MLOps framework built on Databricks and AutoML. These models are actively used to power large scale member decisioning across the Velocity program.

As Velocity accelerates its AI transformation journey, you’ll partner closely with our AI teams to identify, develop and embed AI driven capabilities into scalable products and decisioning frameworks.

In this role you will:

Design, build and deploy statistical and machine learning models using Python and Spark within a modern, scalable data and ML platform, including Databricks, with a focus on deploying models into production environments supporting batch and increasingly near real time decisioning
Own the end to end lifecycle of models, from problem framing and feature engineering through to deployment, monitoring and optimisation
Develop and enhance recommender systems, propensity models and predictive models that drive commercial outcomes
Design and implement experiments (A/B and beyond) to inform decision making, measure causal impact and continuously optimise outcomes
Contribute to and improve MLOps practices, including automation, reproducibility, scalable deployment pipelines and model monitoring
Build scalable data products and reusable decisioning frameworks
Translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights using effective data visualisation and storytelling for both technical and non technical audiences
Partner closely with analytics, engineering and business teams to embed data-driven decision making into workflows
Ensure all work aligns with Virgin Australia Group’s safety, security and resilience standards

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science (PhD desirable)
Around 3+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in a commercial environment
Proficiency in Python, with solid SQL skills and experience working with large datasets (Spark desirable)
Experience working with modern data and ML platforms such as Databricks and/or AWS
A strong foundation in statistics, experimental design and ideally causal inference
Experience working within or contributing to MLOps frameworks
A track record of translating models and analysis into measurable business impact
Strong communication and data visualisation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and drive data-driven decisions
Curiosity, ownership and a drive to automate and scale solutions
Experience in loyalty, travel or consumer products (nice to have)

Why this role is interesting:

Work on production grade machine learning systems that power decisioning at scale
Build on an existing ML and MLOps ecosystem with modern tooling (Databricks, AutoML)
Help shape how experimentation, personalisation and AI driven decisioning are scaled across Velocity

Candidates must be Sydney based, or willing to relocate.

What you’ll get from us 

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to): 

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (plus $1000 travel credits per year) 
Flexible and hybrid working, with a mix of working from home and the office 
‘Dress for Your Day’ – wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you 
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide 
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services 
Free counselling and online tools to support your physical, mental, social and financial health. 

When everyone is included, everyone wins 

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTIQ+ community, and anyone else who wants to join us. 

Ready to apply? 

Working at Virgin Australia isn't just a job. It's a passion that becomes part of you. Are you ready to join us? Get ready for a rewarding experience. Apply today!

Lead the design of a modern technology environment & the development of an enterprise‑wide architecture roadmap for a leading Mental Health Provider
Fixed term 6 month role | Full time
Burnley Location & WFH

 

Mind strongly encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, people with a lived experience of mental ill health and recovery, and applicants from all cultures, genders, sexualities, bodies, abilities, spiritualities, ages and backgrounds.

 

Mind Australia is one of Australia’s largest providers of community-managed psychosocial services, supporting people experiencing mental health and wellbeing challenges to find help, hope and purpose in their lives. Operating for more than 45 years and employing more than 1,300 staff around Australia, Mind is a leading employer of people with lived experience of mental health challenges, recognising their unique ability to connect with and motivate clients and guide recovery.

 

About the role

Mind Australia provides community-based mental health services across Australia, supporting individuals to lead independent, fulfilling lives. The Information Services (IS) enables this mission by delivering robust, innovative, and secure and modern technology platforms that support service delivery, operational efficiency, and data informed decision making and a positive staff experience.

 

We are seeking an experienced, self-motivated Enterprise Architect for a 6 month fixed term opportunity in the Burnley area. You will lead the design of Mind’s modern technology environment and the development of an enterprise‑wide architecture roadmap that strengthens system reliability, reduces technical debt, and enables sustainable digital transformation and organisational growth.

 

Key responsibilities

Shapes and informs enterprise strategy by analysing disruptive forces, business models, and technology trends to guide strategic decisions and investment priorities.
Assesses and prepares the organisation for change through scenario planning and future‑/current‑state modelling to support adaptable, technology‑enabled business strategies.
Translate organisational strategy into actionable technology designs that balance immediate business needs with long‑term sustainability and total cost of ownership
Develop and maintain a technology roadmap that sequences stabilisation, modernisation and transformation activities aligned to organisational strategy and IS priorities
Serve as a trusted advisor to leaders, project teams and vendors, ensuring architectural decisions are transparent, well-governed and support business outcomes

 

What you’ll bring

Tertiary qualifications in Information Systems, Technology, Computer Science, Systems Analysis or equivalent experience
Eight or more years of business experience in strategic and operations planning and/or business analysis or experience as an enterprise architecture consultant.
Demonstrated ongoing professional development in architecture strategy, emerging technologies, and digital transformation
Demonstrated enterprise architecture experience using recognised architecture frameworks and practices, spanning strategy through to execution
Strong breadth across applications, data and analytics, integration patterns, identity & security, cloud platforms and end‑user computing, with practical experience designing Microsoft‑first environments (Azure, M365, Power Platform).

 

Benefits

Salary packaging up to $15,899 p.a. allows you to allocate a portion of your pre-tax income to cover living expenses (e.g. rent, mortgage, child care, car leasing expenses etc.)
Option to access Meal and Entertainment allowance up to $2,650 p.a. via Salary Packaging.
Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP), provided by Converge International, for you and your immediate family members.
Fitness Passport - Access to our corporate health and fitness program, providing you and family with access to a wide choice of fitness facilities.
Hybrid work (choose your days, consistent team collaboration days where possible).

Cross River Rail Delivery Authority (CRRDA)…Your chance to work with the best!

The Cross River Rail Delivery Authority is building a new 10.2km rail line that includes 5.9km of twin tunnels running under the Brisbane River and CBD; with four new underground stations at Boggo Road, Woolloongabba, Albert Street and Roma Street.

Once complete, it will provide trains with a much-needed alternative rail path through the core of the current SEQ Rail network, unlocking a bottleneck and allowing more trains to run more often across the whole of South East Queensland.

The Cross River Rail Delivery Authority is also delivering multiple supporting projects and activities in conjunction with the Department of Transport and Main Roads. These include accessibility rebuilds for eight surface stations, construction of three new stations on the Gold Coast; upgrades for stabling yards; track works and surface rail enhancements; and the introduction of a new ETCS digital signalling system.

 

About the Role

This is a unique opportunity to step into the centre of one of Queensland’s most complex infrastructure programs and shape the insight that informs executive decision making. As a senior member of the Governance and Reporting team the Principal Performance Reporting Lead will operate at the intersection of delivery, program controls and governance, bringing clarity to complexity and helping senior leaders focus on what matters most. This is not a traditional reporting role. It is about judgement, narrative and influence.

You will take complex, and often competing, inputs across cost, schedule, risk and delivery and turn them into clear, decision ready insights. You will frame the issues, highlight the implications and trade-offs, and help leaders understand what action is required.

Working closely with senior stakeholders across the program, you will challenge where needed, align messaging and ensure reporting reflects a coherent, credible and well tested position. Your work will directly support governance forums and key program decisions. You will also play a key role in shaping how and when reporting lands, aligning outputs to governance timing so insights are delivered when they are needed most, not after the fact.

Reporting to the Performance Reporting and Improvements Manager, this role will suit someone who enjoys working in a fast moving, high stakes environment, who can cut through noise, think critically and bring structure, clarity and confidence to complex program performance information. This position would be offered a fixed term contract until August 2028 with possible extension.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the development of executive ready reporting narratives and recommendations that translate complex program performance into clear decision support
Translate program performance into insights that clearly link delivery status to risk, dependencies, commercial impacts and decision consequences.
Apply judgement to identify material issues, prioritise competing inputs and determine appropriate escalation pathways
Work closely with delivery teams, program controls and senior stakeholders to identify, test and refine reporting messages
Challenge inputs constructively to improve clarity, coherence and usefulness of reporting
Plan, manage and adapt integrated reporting and governance cadence so reporting aligns with decision making timelines
Ensure outcomes from governance forums are accurately reflected and tracked through subsequent reporting
Oversee the overall quality and presentation of reporting products, including the effective use of visuals and structure
Drive continuous improvement in reporting and governance processes to improve clarity, timeliness and reduce rework

 

About You:

Strong understanding of project reporting, controls, commercial performance and governance  in complex project environments
Demonstrated ability to synthesise complex information and exercise sound judgement in senior reporting contexts
Proven capability to produce concise, executive ready narratives that clearly frame issues, implications and options
Confidence collaborating with senior stakeholders and delivery teams to shape and challenge reporting constructively
A practical, organised approach and the ability to deliver to tight reporting cycles without compromising quality
Relevant tertiary qualifications or equivalent experience

 

Why work with CRRDA?

On Cross River Rail you will not only work with the best but become your best. And as we enter a new phase of delivery, our project offers a rare career opportunity for top tier professionals to have played their part on something that is genuinely city shaping and transformative.

Our people are supported with flexible work options, career development through the Australian Institute of Management, salary packaging and access to world class employee assistance provider Telus Health.

At Cross River Rail we are an equal opportunity employer and strive to build balanced teams from all walks of life.

 

How To Apply

Please submit your Resume and a short cover letter outlining your suitability referencing the key competencies section of the attached Position Description by clicking on the apply link before the closing date of 7th April 2026.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are strongly encouraged to apply for all Cross River Rail Delivery Authority vacancies.

Job Title: AI Adoption & Enablement Lead or AI Adoption SME
Ad Text:
The AI Adoption & Enablement Lead is responsible for driving organisation-wide adoption, effective usage, and measurable productivity outcomes from enterprise AI tools. The initial focus is on uplifting baseline adoption and competency across the business, followed by the development of role-specific and use‑case‑driven value, particularly for legal and finance users. This role plays a critical part in delivering the CEO-mandated objective of 100% AI adoption by the end of May.

Context

Organisation-wide AI programme currently run by a small, central team.

Primary AI tools in scope include Grok (default AI tool), Copilot (currently web chat), and ChatGPT Enterprise.

Current challenges include low adoption of AI tools, uneven confidence and competency in AI usage, and limited understanding of how to apply LLMs effectively in day-to-day work.

Core user groups include lawyers and finance professionals who are not necessarily technically savvy and require structured enablement.

Key Responsibilities

Adoption & Enablement

Drive organisation-wide adoption of AI tools, with Grok positioned as the default AI platform.

Design and deliver enablement programs that build confidence and practical competency in using AI, including:




How to use large language models (LLMs)

Prompting fundamentals and best practices

Responsible and effective use of AI in professional contexts

Actively support, facilitate, and where required directly run AI training sessions (e.g. workshops, drop-in sessions, roadshows) for individuals and teams.

Develop simple, repeatable education assets (training sessions, guides, examples, playbooks) tailored to non-technical users.

Ensure strong uptake and usage of AI tools already built and available within the business.

Productivity & Measurement

Shift focus from adoption-only metrics to productivity and outcome-based measurement.

Define and track task-based outcomes for individuals and teams.

Measure and report on key usage indicators, including:




Prompt quality

Time spent in AI tools

Hits, sessions, and frequency of use

Use data and insights to continuously refine enablement approaches and prioritise high-impact use cases.

Use Case Development

Initially prioritise broad adoption and baseline competency uplift across all staff.

Progressively identify and develop more specific, role-based and function-specific AI use cases.

Partner closely with legal and finance teams to understand how AI can best support their workflows and decision-making.

Maintain a strong focus on "how we help our users" rather than technology for its own sake.

Stakeholder & Programme Support

Work closely with the central AI programme team to scale impact despite limited resources.

Act as a trusted advisor to business leaders and end users on practical AI adoption.

Support leadership objectives and communicate progress clearly against the CEO’s adoption mandate.

Skills & Experience

Essential

Demonstrated experience driving technology adoption, enablement, or change across a broad user base.

Strong understanding of generative AI and LLMs, with the ability to explain concepts simply and practically.

Proven ability to design and deliver training or enablement for non-technical audiences.

Experience defining and tracking adoption, usage, and productivity metrics.

Highly self-directed and proactive, with the ability to operate effectively in a small team.

Desirable

Experience working with legal, finance, or other professional services users.

Exposure to enterprise AI platforms such as Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, or similar tools.

Background in digital transformation, change management, or capability uplift programs.

Personal Attributes

Self-starter who takes initiative and ownership, avoiding over-reliance on AI or excessive supervision.

Strong communicator who can build trust with non-technical stakeholders.

Outcome-focused, with a clear bias toward practical value and productivity.

Comfortable working in ambiguity and evolving an approach as adoption matures.

Success Measures

Achieving and sustaining near‑100% AI tool adoption across the organisation.

Measurable uplift in employee confidence and competency in using AI.

Clear evidence of productivity improvements tied to task-based outcomes.

Strong engagement and positive feedback from legal and finance user groups.

Job Title: Reporting & Analytics Manager - SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA Transformation
Ad Text:
Our client, a leading ASX-listed enterprise, is undergoing a significant digital transformation - transitioning from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA across multiple business streams.

We’re looking for an experienced Reporting & Analytics Manager to join this high-impact, enterprise-wide program. You will guide the transition of reporting and analytics capabilities from the legacy SAP ECC6 and BW platforms to S/4HANA and associated modern platforms. The current reporting landscape includes SAP, BW, Helix, Power BI, and other tools, with thousands of reports - both sanctioned and unsanctioned - requiring rationalisation, governance, and alignment with future-state architecture.
  
This role requires deep expertise in enterprise reporting, strong business process knowledge, and proven experience managing complex transitions involving multiple platforms and stakeholder groups. The successful candidate will work across business and project teams to define reporting requirements, establish governance, and ensure a smooth and value-driven transition to S/4HANA.
  
Essential Experience:

10+ years of experience in enterprise reporting and analytics leadership.
Proven success in managing reporting transitions in SAP environments
Strong knowledge of reporting and analytics platforms including SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP BDC SAP BW, Power BI, and other reporting platforms.
Experience in data governance, rationalisation, and stakeholder engagement.
Ability to navigate complex reporting landscapes and drive simplification.
 
Desirable:

Experience with SAP BTP and embedded analytics.
Experience with SAP Group Reporting deployments leveraging SAP analytics cloud.
Familiarity with infrastructure, services, or asset-heavy sectors.
  
Responsibilities:
Lead the transition from ECC & BW to S/4HANA and modernised analytics platforms.
Define and document current and future-state reporting requirements.
Align reporting approach with SAP best practices and fit-to-standard principles.
Engage stakeholders and platform owners (SAP, BW, Helix, Power BI) to support design, testing, and readiness.
Drive adoption of standardised reporting and governance.
Protect critical reporting functions during transition.
Establish reporting and analytics governance frameworks.
Rationalise existing reports to align with business needs.
Support clean core strategy by minimising customisations.
Coordinate with workstream leads on shared resources and dependencies.
Lead reporting cutover and go-live planning.
Ensure business readiness and support alignment across units.

Role Details:
Location: North Sydney - Hybrid (3 days in-office / 2 days WFH)
Duration: 12-month contract with strong extension potential
Rate: $1200–$1,400/day (including super), commensurate with experience
Start: ASAP, with interview slots available this week

“It begins at Bendigo Bank”

 

We’ve never been ‘just a bank’. Just like you should never be ‘just an employee’. We’re united in our belief that in banking, better can be bigger, and together we’re making it happen.

 

It starts here. With Bendigo Bank… and you.



About the role

This is a pivotal opportunity to go beyond traditional oversight and take ownership of our Group's Conduct Risk framework. More than just a policy-holder, you will be a trusted advisor to our most senior leaders, using your expertise to influence key decisions and shape our approach to risk across the entire organisation.

If you are a seasoned risk professional with a passion for driving meaningful change and the credibility to influence at an executive level, this is your chance to play a crucial leadership role in our journey.

Your responsibilities will include:

Own the Framework: Take full ownership of the design, implementation, and ongoing effectiveness of the Group's Conduct Risk Policy, ensuring it is embedded and effective across all business divisions.
Act as a Strategic Advisor: Serve as the key subject matter expert on conduct risk, providing critical insights and influential advice to senior management committees and key governance forums.
Drive Data-Led Insights: Develop and manage sophisticated risk reporting, including data stewardship for our conduct risk appetite and key risk indicators, to ensure accurate and actionable intelligence.
Lead with Influence: Proactively identify and lead targeted deep-dive reviews. Although you won't have direct reports, you will be a core leader within the team, mentoring junior members and spearheading initiatives to uplift culture and capability.
Collaborate for Success: Work closely with a wide range of senior stakeholders across all three Lines of Accountability to ensure a cohesive and robust approach to managing conduct risk.



 This is a permanent, full-time opportunity, ideally located at our Melbourne office, but we are open to Sydney, or Adelaide for the right person. We believe in putting our people first, which is why we offer flexible, hybrid work options with a minimum of 3 days in the office per week.

 


About you

We're looking for a passionate go-getter with:

Experience in the Banking and Finance sector, bringing a strong background in Conduct Risk, non-financial risk, and compliance frameworks.
Proven ability to operate and influence at the most senior levels, with exceptional stakeholder management skills and experience partnering strategically with Executive and Senior Management.
Demonstrable experience in designing and implementing practical risk frameworks and leading large-scale projects or change programs from a strategic and practical standpoint.
A strategic and critical thinker with outstanding problem-solving skills, who thrives in a complex and agile environment, effectively managing competing priorities and driving continuous improvement.



 

So, why work with us?

You’ll also get access to a great range of benefits, including:

Looking for hybrid work, flexible hours, or a compressed week? Our flexible work options are designed to fit your lifestyle.
Health and well-being support, including discounted gym memberships, private health insurance options, and our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for you and your immediate family members.
Take your learning to the next level through opportunities like our corporate university ‘BEN U’ or at an external provider of your choice.


We believe a diverse workforce supported by an inclusive culture is central to our success and we actively encourage applications from those who bring diversity of thought to our business. We support candidate requests for adjustment to accommodate an illness, injury, or disability to equitably participate in the selection process.

Seeking a Lead Data Engineer with Experience working with distributed data processing technologies (e.g., Spark or equivalent)

Lead Data Engineer for Data Analytics

The Department requires data engineering capability to design and evolve analytics capability across a hybrid environment, encompassing continued development of on‑premises analytics capability alongside establishment of a modern cloud-based analytics platform. The role has a strong emphasis on production-grade engineering, and DevOps practices. It delivers priority data pipelines using a medallion-style architecture to support advanced analytics and AI use cases.

Key duties and responsibilities

The Lead Data Engineer for Data Analytics will:

Work collaboratively with a small multidisciplinary team to stand up a cloud analytics platform that enables analytics and advanced analytics use cases, using contemporary cloud-native and hybrid data engineering patterns (e.g., Azure, Microsoft Fabric).
Design and implement priority data pipelines aligned to a Medallion-style layered architecture, supporting repeatable ingestion, transformation and analytics workflows.
Implement modern DataOps/DevOps practices for data engineering, including CI/CD for pipelines and notebooks, infrastructure-as-code, environment promotion, automated testing, release management, and operational runbooks.
Implement monitoring/observability and operational controls, including logging, alerting, data quality checks, lineage capture, and metadata management patterns aligned to governance tools (e.g., Microsoft Purview).
Build scalable data engineering solutions using distributed processing technologies, such as Spark‑based compute engines, appropriate to the hybrid environments.
Apply awareness of enterprise integration patterns (e.g., streaming ingestion, mirroring/CDC, event‑driven orchestration, hybrid data movement and virtualisation patterns) to deliver robust and reliable integration across cloud and on‑premise environments.
Implement reusable patterns, standards and templates to support consistent analytics delivery across datasets and use cases.
Collaborate closely with architects, analysts and data science practitioners to enable analytics and data science capability on top of shared, well-governed data foundations.
Operate effectively within secure or sensitive environments, applying appropriate security, privacy and compliance considerations when designing and delivering data engineering solutions.
Contribute to uplift of analytics engineering capability through documentation, knowledge sharing and pragmatic, maintainable design choices.
Desirable skills and qualifications include:

Experience working within modern Azure, Microsoft Fabric and Purview ecosystems, with an understanding of cloud‑native analytics and governance capabilities.
Experience enabling data science and advanced analytics capability on cloud or on-prem data platforms.
Experience working with graph databases for graph-enabled analytics.
Experience applying enterprise integration patterns (e.g., streaming ingestion, CDC/mirroring, event‑driven orchestration, hybrid integration or virtualisation) to support reliable data movement and interoperability across systems.
Experience applying data mesh type architectural patterns to support governed, scalable and domain‑aligned data products.
Experience with MLOps.
Experience working within an Intelligence context.
Essential criteria

Demonstrated experience designing and building modern analytics platforms that support analytics use cases.
Strong experience implementing data pipelines and data transformations for repeatable ingestion and curation workflows.
Demonstrated experience with modern data engineering toolchains in cloud environments, with skills transferable across cloud providers.
Experience working with distributed data processing technologies (e.g., Spark or equivalent) to develop scalable batch or streaming data solutions.
Demonstrated ability to translate analytical and data science requirements into robust data engineering solutions.

About the role:

We are seeking a senior Engagement Lead to oversee our ANAPLAN capability within our Regional Corporate and Institutional Banking division (GCIB APAC).

This role will report directly to the CFO of Global Corporate & Institutional Banking APAC (GCIB APAC) and will work closely with the broader Financial Planning and Data Analytics Teams to ensure the seamless delivery of ANAPLAN as GCIB APAC’s ERP tool for planning, forecasting and reporting requirements.

Within GCIB, ANAPLAN has been selected as the preferred ERP to deliver integrated planning, forecasting and reporting information and insights to its business leaders across 13 countries, seven product groups and eight regional shared service functions. Ensuring that ANAPLAN remains a reliable and effective platform to simplify and standardize the planning, forecasting and reporting processes is a critical enabler to GCIB’s longer term effectiveness and efficiency aspirations.

The Engagement Lead oversees the overall Anaplan support engagement, ensuring client satisfaction, operational excellence, and continuous business growth. This role is the senior-most escalation point for complex technical or strategic issues, while also leading governance, QA, and new opportunity identification

What you’ll be doing:

Own the business relationship and act as the primary point of contact for strategic discussions
Govern the overall support delivery — SLA performance, capacity management, and quality assurance
Undertake monthly/fortnightly BAU system-related maintenance such as taking down of snapshots, scheduling of data loads, publishing of reports to end consumers and opening the forecasting environments to users
Liaising with Compliance/ASO/BI to ensure adherence to regulatory compliance and data governance processes and controls
Lead resolution of the most complex or critical technical issues
Review and approve solution designs, ensuring adherence to Anaplan best practices
Identify new opportunities for enhancement projects, upgrades, and extensions of Anaplan solutions
Mentor planning teams to support their development in terms of process simplification, standardization and automation
Collaborate with leadership on business growth, client proposals, and support model improvement

What we are looking for

10+ years of experience including 5+ years of System Engagement Management experience with proven delivery and client management background
Bachelor's or master’s degree in Finance, Economics, Information Management and Technology, Engineering, or related fields
Certified Anaplan Solution Architect or Master Anaplanner (preferred)
Deep understanding of end-to-end planning, forecasting and reporting processes within a global corporate and investment bank
Strong leadership, stakeholder management, and communication skills
Experience managing multi-client or multi-region support operations
Background in financial/ multi-dimensional modelling, analytics, or business intelligence
Experience in a project team environment with structured methodologies for business requirements, processes, data conversions, and system interfaces
Experience formulating and developing implementation strategies for business requirements and sustainable solutions
Experience with Data Integration
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with a passion for numbers and cause-effect relationships
Able to work autonomously, make decisions, and take responsibility
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
Willingness to travel and collaborate in a multi-cultural environment

Our culture:

MUFG fosters a highly professional, supportive work environment that enables our employees to help maximize our customers' prosperity. We focus on building long-term customer relationships and giving back to our communities in order to develop shared and sustainable growth for a better world.

Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) is an equal opportunity employer. We view our employees as our key assets as they are fundamental to our long-term growth and success. MUFG is committed to hiring based on merit and organsational fit, regardless of race, religion or gender.

Job Description:

The Senior Data Steward is a foundational member of Cotality’s Data Governance team, reporting directly to the Director of Data Governance. In this role, you will help operationalise enterprise governance frameworks and embed stewardship practices across federated business domains. You will work closely with data owners, product teams, legal, compliance, and technology stakeholders to ensure that data assets are discoverable, documented, and responsibly managed.

As Cotality evolves its governance model, this role will be instrumental in defining metadata standards, supporting data quality initiatives, and enabling business teams to take ownership of their data. You’ll help build a culture of data accountability and literacy, ensuring that governance is not just a policy but a practice.

What You Will Be Doing:

Operationalise data governance frameworks by implementing stewardship practices across federated business domains.

Define and maintain metadata standards, business glossaries, and lineage documentation for critical data assets.

Collaborate with data owners and domain leads to establish clear roles, responsibilities, and accountability for data quality and usage.

Support the adoption of governance technologies (e.g., Collibra, data catalogues) to ensure discoverability and trust in data assets.

Ensure compliance with data privacy laws, ethical use principles, and contractual obligations for sensitive datasets.

Educate and enable business teams through training, documentation, and ongoing support to build data literacy and stewardship capability.

Contribute to governance forums and working groups, sharing insights and promoting best practices across the organisation.

Job Qualifications:

What You Will Bring:

Essential:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business, Law, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

Minimum 3+ years’ experience in data stewardship, data governance, or data management roles.

Strong understanding of metadata management, data lineage, and data quality principles.

Familiarity with governance frameworks such as DAMA, DMBoK, or DCAM.

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into business value.

Preferred:

Experience with data catalog tools such as Collibra, Alation, or Informatica.

Ad Text:
Are you ready to play a critical role in CS Energy’s transition journey? As our Climate and Sustainability Reporting Lead, you’ll be part of the Energy Transition Team, sitting at the centre of our climate risk, Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) and sustainability reporting uplift. In this role, you will help equip the business with the insights, data integrity and reporting frameworks needed to navigate a rapidly evolving regulatory landscape and transition confidently toward a lower emissions future. This is a high impact role for a technical sustainability specialist who thrives on elevating reporting quality, improving emissions accuracy and supporting enterprise wide climate capability.

Working closely with the Head of Sustainability and stakeholders across the organisation, you will lead our ESG disclosure program, strengthen emissions reporting, support decarbonisation planning and ensure CS Energy continues to meet emerging AASB, Queensland Treasury and national climate disclosure expectations. If you’re passionate about translating complex sustainability data into clear, credible, decision ready insights, this could be your next career defining opportunity.

Duties include but are not limited to:

Leading the development and delivery of CS Energy’s ESG and sustainability reporting program, including GRI aligned double materiality assessments.

Monitoring and interpreting emerging sustainability frameworks and regulatory developments to ensure ongoing compliance.

Overseeing Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions measurement and reporting, aligned to GHG Protocol and evolving mandatory reporting requirements.

Driving continuous improvement of data collection systems, methodologies, controls and emissions reporting processes.

Coordinating organisation wide climate related risk assessments, both transition and physical and supporting integration into enterprise risk frameworks.

Supporting the development, analysis and tracking of emissions reduction targets, pathways and decarbonisation performance.

Identifying and assessing ESG risks and opportunities, ensuring robust governance and reporting mechanisms.

Delivering training, guidance and internal capability uplift in sustainability reporting and climate related literacy.

Collaborating across Governance, Risk, Operations, Asset Management and Corporate Communications to ensure consistent, transparent and high quality sustainability disclosures.

Preparing insights and information for Queensland Treasury, auditors, industry bodies and other external stakeholders.

About You

What’s in it for you

Be part of a high-performing team that values collaboration and continuous improvement.

Professional development opportunities, including exposure to training systems and compliance frameworks. 

Competitive remuneration and benefits, with a focus on work life balance. 


To be successful in this role you will have the following skills and experience: 

Relevant tertiary qualifications in environmental science, sustainability, engineering, energy, commerce, economics or related discipline.

Postgraduate qualifications in climate risk, sustainability, ESG reporting, data analytics or a related field are highly desirable.

Additional relevant certifications (e.g., GHG accounting, climate‑risk analysis, GRI sustainability reporting) are advantageous but not essential.

Reporting, including application of recognised methodologies such as the GHG Protocol.

Demonstrated experience delivering sustainability, ESG and or climate‑related reporting, preferably within an emissions‑intensive or complex operational environment.

Strong business acumen, analytical capability and communication skills, with demonstrated ability to interpret complex sustainability and climate‑related data and translate insights for diverse stakeholders.

Familiarity with sustainability and climate‑disclosure frameworks (e.g GRI, SASB, TCFD, AASB S2) and an understanding of relevant regulatory and compliance expectations in Queensland.

Proven ability to collaborate effectively across functions and influence stakeholders to support sustainability outcomes, uplift capability and embed improved reporting practices.

About Us

CS Energy is a proudly Queensland-owned and based energy company that provides power to some of our state’s biggest industries and employers. We generate and sell electricity, we are an energy retailer to commercial and industrial businesses, and we are investing in new energy assets including renewable hydrogen, solar and wind farms, and battery storage.  

Every voice is important to us  
 
CS Energy is proud to foster an environment that builds respect, promotes inclusiveness, celebrates diversity and embraces the unique skills and qualities of all our employees. Reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process will be supplied to those who require additional support. 

As part of the recruitment process, the successful applicant may be required to complete a number of background checks including; reference checks, pre-employment medical, national police checks, right to work eligibility, verification of qualifications, and bankruptcy checks.

Principal ERP Functional Specialists - (Fixed Assets/Projects)
 

Multiple Permanent opportunities available  
$173,345.00  - $193,906.00 pa (plus 12% superannuation and annual leave loading)  
Opportunity to work from our modern Chatswood Office  
Salary packaging, up to 12 extra days off per year, fitness passport and more!  


Applications Close: 11:59pm, Monday 6th April 2026


We are looking for Principal ERP Functional Specialists across Oracle Fusion Fixed Assets, Projects, and Receivables, and Pathology Billing applications to join a high-performing team at the forefront of digital transformation in healthcare with eHealth NSW.  

NSW Health is transitioning to Oracle Fusion ERP, known internally as StaffLink ERP. The eHealth Corporate Applications team oversees all StaffLink ERP modules, where you will act as a leader, partnering with senior stakeholders to deliver funded projects, drive innovation, and enhance decision making and financial transparency across one of Australia’s largest health systems. 


Role 1: Principal ERP Functional Specialist – Fixed Assets 
The Fixed Assets Specialist is responsible for implementing and supporting Oracle Fusion Fixed Assets functionality across NSW Health. As a key technical and strategic leader, this role will drive optimisation initiatives within the Oracle ERP Finance platform to ensure solutions are aligned with organisational objectives and industry best practice.

The Principal ERP Specialist partners with the ERP Platform Owners and senior stakeholders to drive innovation, shape the ERP roadmap, and deliver solutions that improve financial transparency, decision-making, and overall business performance. 

In this role, you will:  

Lead strategic optimisations for Fixed Assets within Oracle ERP Finance.  
Act as an enterprise ERP lead, partnering with senior stakeholders to shape the ERP roadmap.  
Deliver funded projects and continuous improvement initiatives.  
Investigate, design, configure, test, and support Oracle ERP applications both Fusion Cloud and R12 environments. 



Role 2: Principal ERP Functional Specialist – Projects 
The Projects Specialist is responsible for implementing the suite of Project Applications and providing support and system enhancements to NSW Health's user base. You will be accountable for the delivery of strategic optimizations for the suite of Project applications in the Oracle ERP Finance platform. This role ensures that ERP applications are developed according to best practice, and in alignment with corporate objectives. 

In this role, you will:  

Drive enhancements for Oracle ERP Project applications.  
Collaborate across Finance and IT to deliver scalable solutions that support statewide operations.  
Provide leadership on ERP roadmap and innovation initiatives.  
Investigate, design, configure, test, and support Oracle ERP applications (StaffLink ERP and StaffLink R12). 


To be successful in role 1 or role 2, we are looking for passionate leaders with the ability to understand complex information including:

Understanding of the core Finance Processes with hands-on experience of Fixed Assets or Oracle Projects        
Extensive experience in Oracle ERP functional roles (Cloud) with R12 grounding.
Experience integrating data between Oracle modules using API's, Invoice Import, WebADI, FBDI, and More4Apps loaders.  
Ability to enforce governance standards and oversee ERP design in large enterprises.
Knowledge of Fusion ERP OCI, ATP, APIs, and integrations is a bonus. 

These roles are internally referred to as Principal Digital Specialist – ERP 
 

How to apply
Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience in the key business area/s required for the role.

We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we accept their use, we’re eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application!  

For further discussions around the role or recruitment process, including adjustments, please contact our Talent Advisor at Olivia.liu@health.nsw.gov.au or Hiring Manager Anthony Robidashvily at Anthony.Robidashvily@health.nsw.gov.au.

In 2025 SPA acquired Proximity Advisory Services, a well-established and highly regarded consultancy organisation with unrivalled legal abilities, exceptional commercial capability, and a raft of specialisations in consulting. Today, SPA has a global footprint, including more than 130 employees based across Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney and Adelaide.  

SPA Australia’s Defence & Analytics team provides senior decision-makers within the Australian Department of Defence (Defence) with timely, objective assessments that inform capability employment and investment strategies. 

Our team of former military professionals and operations research analysts integrates deep operational expertise with advanced analytic methodologies—including force generation modelling, agent-based tactical simulations, and operational mission analyses—to deliver trusted decision support that directly contributes to national security outcomes. 

SPA Australia offers decision-makers within the Australian Defence Force evidence-based insights that address capacity and capability gaps, strengthen operational effectiveness, and optimise the deployment of current, and future Defence capabilities. 

SPA seeks a passionate and dedicated Director to join our Defence & Analytics practice. This senior role provides strategic leadership to high-performance, cross-functional teams conducting advanced quantitative and qualitative analyses of Australian and adversary military operations and capabilities across all warfighting domains, missions, and functional areas. 

The Director will lead multiple concurrent workstreams—often under tight timelines—while rapidly assimilating new information, technologies, and concepts. The role requires the ability to anticipate emerging analytical needs, balance immediate customer priorities, and consistently deliver rigorous, defensible insights that inform senior decision-makers at strategic and operational levels. 

This role will suit an experienced defence professional with demonstrated fluency across priority military operations and the enabling capabilities that underpin them. The successful candidate will also play a critical role in business development, leveraging existing networks to identify opportunities, strengthen client relationships, and contribute to the growth of the Defence & Analytics practice.

Key Responsibilities: 

Provide strategic leadership to cross-functional teams conducting complex analysis across all warfighting domains assessing Australian and adversary military operations, capabilities and capacities. 

Foster a mission-focused, high-performance culture and provide strong leadership to project teams. 

Demonstrate and apply deep expertise in military operations and support and enabling capability roadmaps 

Build high level relationships with senior Defence stakeholders and be a trusted advisor. 

Support Defence clients in the planning, design, and execution of experimentation and operational analysis aligned with joint force design and integration priorities.  

Facilitate stakeholder engagements, including workshops and demonstrations. 

Oversee multiple concurrent analytical workstreams, ensuring timely delivery of rigorous, defensible analysis that supports strategic and operational decision-making. 

Write capability analysis reports and briefs and present results to stakeholders from a wide range of Defence organisation and varying levels of domain expertise 

Support clients in implementing operational and tactical plans that contribute to national security objectives. 

Identify strategic growth opportunities across the joint domain and lead growth within the defence joint operations market.  

Required Experience: 

Must be an Australian citizen

15 + years relevant Australia Defence and Australia Federal Government experience in military operations and associated technologies

Demonstrated expertise in providing one or more warfighting domains, missions, or functional areas to the Australia Defence Force

Background in capability development, systems engineering, operations or mission analysis

Proven ability to conduct rigorous research and analysis and form defensible conclusions using available, often incomplete, information

Strong written and verbal communication skills, producing compelling and clear analytic products and briefings to support senior-leadership decision-making

Experience leading small teams and projects in support of Australian Defence capabilities, or Australia Federal Government key projects

Demonstrated ability to apply innovative analytical concepts, quantitative tools, and data visualisation methods to military problem sets and present results to executive-level leaders

Experience in Australian military operational planning within an operational headquarters or in Australian Defence capability management (including requirements definition, capability documentation, modelling and simulation, or operations research)

International Defence cooperation initiatives experience highly regarded

Current Negative Vetting 2 (NV2) Australian security clearance, or willingness and ability to obtain and maintain one

Desired Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in a STEM discipline; or substantial military experience plus an advanced non-technical degree and proven Defence capability acumen (e.g., completion of the Capability Technology Management Program or equivalent) 

Current Positive Vetting (PV) Australian security clearance

Note: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) and/or Export Administration Regulations (EAR) are applicable to this role and as such, the position will or is likely to require access to material controlled by ITAR and EAR (Controlled Material).  Therefore, any person occupying the position must be able to satisfy ITAR and EAR controls, which may require specific authorisation to access Controlled Materials.  Accordingly, your citizenship, previous citizenships, race or nationality, or substantive contacts where such contacts are affiliated with proscribed countries under ITAR may be a factor in determining your suitability for this role. Further information on this requirement can be found at: https://spa.com/about/compliance-statements/.
